title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Highlights: Nasdaq jumps over 1% as chip stocks rally, investors eye AI earnings - The Economic Times
text:
Dow Jones Today | US Stock Market Highlights: U.S. stocks climbed as chipmakers led gains on AI-driven optimism and a strong earnings outlook. Broadcom surged on an extended Apple deal, while Microsoft fell after job cuts. Investors remain cautious on Fed policy ahead of minutes and upcoming earnings.

Gold prices slipped from a two-week peak as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on demand, though losses were limited by signs of cooling U.S. labour market conditions that tempered expectations of near-term Federal Reserve rate hikes.

Investors are now awaiting the Fed’s latest meeting minutes for policy cues, with markets pricing in a roughly 57% chance of a September rate hike. Other metals were mixed, with silver and platinum declining while palladium edged higher.

U.S. natural gas futures climbed to a one-week high, supported by declining production and increased flows to LNG export plants. Prices settled at $3.245 per mmBtu, while market spreads signaled limited near-term supply concerns despite elevated inventories.

Output in the Lower 48 eased slightly, while demand remained firm amid above-normal temperatures expected through July. LNG feedgas flows increased month-on-month, helping offset ample storage levels, which are projected to remain over 6% above the five-year average.

A senior Iranian security official cautioned former U.S. President Donald Trump to address the Iranian people respectfully, warning that failure to do so would prompt a response “in another language,” signaling a potential escalation in rhetoric.

South Korea began 24-hour trading of the won, marking a key step in opening its financial markets to global investors. The currency held steady against the dollar after a slight gain on the first day of extended trading.

A foreign exchange dealer was seen at Hana Bank’s trading room in Seoul on July 6 as the new regime took effect, highlighting the country’s effort to boost accessibility and liquidity in its currency markets, Bloomberg reported.

- Thomas Hayes, ⁠chairman at Great Hill Capital LLC

Lone Star Funds has agreed to acquire ContiTech, the material solutions unit of Continental AG, in a deal valued at €4 billion. The transaction also includes performance-based payments of up to €250 million in the coming years.

A rebound in AI-related stocks pushed the S&P 500 up 0.7%, bringing it within 1% of its record high, even as most stocks declined. Gains in tech lifted the Nasdaq 1.1%, while the Dow edged slightly higher. Broadcom led advances after a chip deal with Apple, while TeraWulf jumped on an AI data center agreement.

Investor focus remains on the sustainability of AI-driven valuations, with upcoming listings like SK Hynix set to test demand. Meanwhile, oil prices eased on higher OPEC+ output, and Treasury yields stayed largely steady amid mixed economic signals.

U.S. crude inventories in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve fell by 6.2 million barrels to 319.5 million in the week ended July 3, marking the lowest level since April 1983, according to Department of Energy data. The decline is part of a planned 172 million barrel release to offset supply disruptions linked to the Iran war and ease fuel prices.

Since the conflict began in late February, total U.S. oil inventories, including commercial and SPR stocks, have dropped by about 120.7 million barrels to 734 million, the lowest level since 1984.

Aluminium prices rose on Monday as focus ​returned to forecasts of shortages ​created by disruptions to supplies from the Middle East and ​dwindling stocks. Benchmark aluminium on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.8% at $3,115 a metric ton at 1603 GMT. It hit $3,040, the lowest since February 19, as peace talks between the United States ‌and Iran ⁠fuelled optimism ⁠for a resumption of supplies from the Middle East — housing 9% of global aluminium smelting capacity.

But ​even if shipments through the Strait of Hormuz restart, the market is still likely ​to see a deficit this year.

Shares in easyJet ​hit four-year highs on Monday after the British budget airline agreed in principle to a £5.5 billion ($7.34 billion) takeover from U.S. investor Castlelake, though caution over regulatory hurdles capped market gains.

EasyJet said on Sunday it was open to Castlelake's sweetened bid of £6.90 per share, a big jump from the U.S. firm's opening gambit of £5.60, suggesting winding negotiations could finally see the London-listed carrier be taken private.

Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said forward guidance can be a valuable tool that helps speed up the impact of monetary policy, noting that signals in 2021 pushed market rates higher even before actual Fed hikes. He said such communication can strengthen policymaking when used under the right conditions.

However, Waller cautioned that guidance can become counterproductive if applied too rigidly, citing the Fed’s delay in raising rates until March 2022 despite earlier signals. He added that in uncertain economic environments, where multiple outcomes are possible, it may be better for central banks to avoid using forward guidance altogether.

- David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation

President Donald Trump said on Monday the United States would either reach an agreement with Iran or “finish the job,” adding that his preference remains a negotiated deal.

The dollar edged higher after last week’s weak U.S. jobs data reduced expectations of a near-term Federal Reserve rate hike, while the yen hovered near a 40-year low around 162 per dollar, keeping markets alert to possible Japanese intervention. Softer payroll growth and lower oil prices eased rate hike bets, though uncertainty remains ahead of upcoming Fed minutes. Analysts expect the yen to stay under pressure due to interest rate differentials, with any intervention likely offering only temporary support.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday signalled that bitcoin could potentially be included in the administration’s proposed Trump Accou
